response: the river flow moves pollution downstream while diffusion spreads it out and decay reduces levels over time. solving the equation with given values shows concentration drops as distance increases. placing three treatment units at specific spots along the ten kilometer stretch will lower the final amount of toxin. optimal sites are found by balancing the reduction factor against the remaining distance to the intake. calculations suggest spacing them to maximize the combined effect of exponential decay. this strategy helps protect the town water supply from industrial waste. the plan aims to clean the stream before it reaches the village pipes. we must act fast to stop the spread of harmful chemicals in our local river. cancel
